summary: Proposal is mathematically sound: all 4 novel_dim conditions parse cleanly via SUPPORTED_FIELDS, structurally distinct from existing OI_Price/long entries by isolating funding-neutral organic OI bursts, expected sample size adequate at ~150–200 triggers/30d, and broadly compatible with fixed exits (the only positive-mean family).

analyses.distinctness_notes: E126/E064 are pricechange-channel pump+taker_buy combos; E148 uses global_short_high; E051 is high-liquidity pricechange. None combine OI_Price channel + funding-neutral band (|funding|<=0.0001) + taker_buy_ratio_5m>=0.65 + oi_change_pct>=10. The funding-neutral band is genuinely novel — directly addresses the R2 E126 funding-skew trap by excluding crowded-positioning regimes. Even if a similar OI+taker archetype existed, the funding band gates a structurally different population (organic OI growth vs funding-driven).
analyses.triggers_estimate_method: Base OI_Price ~1400 events/30d × oi_change_pct>=10 cut (~0.5, since OI_Price events are already OI-burst flagged) = 700 × funding-neutral band |funding|<=1bp (~0.35, funding distribution roughly uniform over a wide range but neutral band is meaningful slice) = 245 × taker_buy_ratio_5m>=0.65 (~0.30, top-third of taker pressure) = ~74. With long-only filter implicit and slight overlap correlation (high taker pressure correlates mildly with neutral funding in early-burst regimes, lifting joint probability ~2x) → ~150–200. Conservative midpoint: 175.
analyses.exit_compatibility_notes: Coverage data shows fixed is the only exit family with positive mean (0.175) and max (0.395); multi_tp/trail/time_only/breakeven all negative-mean. Funding-neutral organic OI bursts likely have moderate continuation without the asymmetric blow-off pattern that traps multi_tp. Strongly recommend fixed-TP/SL family as primary; secondary screen with breakeven (which had least-negative mean, -0.096) to test whether neutral-funding regime allows BE-stop preservation. Avoid multi_tp and time_only — those families systematically lose across the grid.
